title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation
description: A cache key includes every viewer header, authorization is omitted, the origin receives different data than the cache varies on, and releases rely on global invalidation as routine deployment. Learners cumulatively produce cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
